related_to: Data-driven verification, Safe & robust robot control
discovered_from: Contingency-Aware Planning via Certified Neural HJ Reachability (2603.17022); Formal Methods for Robotic Manipulation survey (hal-05533865)
notes: Hamilton-Jacobi reachability provides formal safety guarantees by solving HJ PDEs to compute safe sets. Appeared in multiple papers this week including a neural operator approach to accelerate HJ computation. Closely related to data-driven verification and formal safety methods.

related_to: Data-driven verification, Safe & robust robot control
discovered_from: A General Framework for Robust Quantitative Semantics of STL (chen2026general); STLts-Div (2603.17293); Formal Methods for Robotic Manipulation survey
notes: STL is a widely used specification language for cyber-physical systems and robotics. Three papers this week deal with STL quantitative semantics and trace synthesis. Most relevant as a specification tool for verification tasks; may not be core interest but worth monitoring.

related_to: Data-driven verification, Safe & robust robot control, Verification of visuomotor policies
discovered_from: Topological Obstructions to CBFs (2603.18422); Integral CBFs with Input Delay (2603.24566); Enforcing Mixed Constraints with Backup CBFs (2603.17097); Constricting Tubes CBF (2603.17003); Safety Guarantees via Constrained RL (IEEE 11430591)
notes: CBFs are a dominant method for safety-critical control and appear in 6+ papers this week across CBF theory, extensions to input delay, mixed constraints, and neural controllers. Very closely related to core interest in data-driven verification and safe robot control. May warrant explicit tracking as a method.

related_to: Generalist policies, imitation learning, reinforcement learning
notes: Generative world models increasingly used for policy evaluation, improvement, and planning. Relevant to verification if used for safety analysis.

related_to: Data-driven verification, verification of visuomotor policies, HJ reachability
discovered_from: Latent Safety Filters (RSS 2025), V-MORALS, hierarchical reachability verification papers
notes: Performing reachability and safety analysis in learned latent spaces rather than raw state spaces. Directly relevant to verifying vision-based and high-dimensional systems.

related_to: Vision-Language-Action models, generalist policies
notes: Growing trend of adding explicit reasoning (textual or multimodal CoT) to VLA models. May become relevant to verification if reasoning traces can be formally checked.

related_to: Data-driven verification, safe robot control, VLA models
discovered_from: SABER (black-box VLA attacks), Altered Thoughts (CoT vulnerabilities), Diffusion Policy Attacker papers
notes: Testing and ensuring robustness of learned robot policies against adversarial perturbations. Closely related to verification - identifying failure modes.

related_to: Data-driven verification, Safe & robust robot control
discovered_from: Conformal Koopman for Embedded Nonlinear Control (2603.21580); Data-Driven Reachability of Nonlinear Lipschitz Systems via Koopman (2604.00150)
notes: Koopman operator lifting recurs as a tool for both data-driven reachability and statistically robust nonlinear control. Two papers this week use it explicitly. May warrant explicit tracking as a method given closeness to data-driven verification.

related_to: Verification of visuomotor policies, Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs), Safe & robust robot control
discovered_from: Speculative Verification for VLA (2604.02965); Causal Scene Narration with Runtime Safety Supervision for VLA Driving (2604.01723); Dreaming the Unseen (2603.21017)
notes: Multiple papers this week add runtime verification or Simplex-style fallback supervision around learned (VLA, diffusion) policies. Distinct from offline verification — focuses on online checking and arbitration. Highly relevant to data-driven verification of black-box controllers.

related_to: Safe & robust robot control, Conformal prediction, Control Barrier Functions (CBFs)
discovered_from: RL for Risk Adaptation via Differentiable CVaR Barrier Functions (2605.21257); Differentiable Optimization Layered Safety-Critical Control for Risk-Aware Navigation via Conformal Prediction (2605.16327)
notes: Two papers this week explicitly use CVaR / risk measures inside the safety layer (CBF or differentiable optimization). Risk-aware formulations are an emerging method for trading off safety conservatism in learned controllers and tie directly to conformal-prediction-style risk control.

related_to: Imitation learning, Reinforcement learning, Generalist policies
discovered_from: NavRL++ Sim-to-Real RL Navigation (2605.15559); Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation (2605.09789)
notes: Recurring theme of analyzing sim-to-real gap design choices, distinct from policy/method papers. Tangential to verification but a frequent topic in alerts; may be worth tracking lightly.

related_to: Verification of visuomotor policies, Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs), Latent-space safety analysis
discovered_from: Event-Grounded Sparse Autoencoders for VLA Policies (2605.17204)
notes: Adapting mechanistic interpretability tools (sparse autoencoders, feature steering) from language models to VLAs. Directly relevant to verification of black-box visuomotor policies and to latent-space safety analysis. Currently a single strong paper but a growing thread worth tracking explicitly.

related_to: Verification of visuomotor policies, Diffusion models, Generalist policies
discovered_from: VERITAS (2606.18247); ViTaL/Inference-time Policy Steering via Vision and Touch (2606.14981); CTRL-STEER (2606.00269); UniIntervene (2606.12372)
notes: Recurring this week: verifying/steering pre-trained generative policies at inference time (visual, tactile, activation-space, agentic intervention). Closely tied to verification of visuomotor policies and to Sumanths data-driven verification focus.

interest_name: Uncertainty quantification & failure detection for learned policies
related_to: Conformal prediction, Verification of visuomotor policies, Adversarial robustness of robot policies, Runtime verification of learned policies
discovered_from: Uncertainty Quantification for Flow-Based VLA (2606.18043); SC3-Eval test-time consistency (2606.18610); Can Predicted Dynamics Exist (2606.00089)
notes: Three papers this week build explicit uncertainty/failure-detection signals around learned policies (epistemic UQ for flow VLAs, self-consistency uncertainty for world-model evaluators, physical-admissibility checks). Distinct from conformal prediction per se; a recurring deployment-time verification theme worth tracking.

related_to: Adversarial robustness of robot policies|Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs)|Verification of visuomotor policies
discovered_from: gupta-2026-vlas-fail (SafeContract black-box monitoring toolkit), cui-2026-libero-safety (LIBERO-Safety benchmark for VLA physical/semantic safety)
notes: Emerging theme: dedicated benchmarks and toolkits for systematically evaluating safety properties of learned robot policies (VLAs, diffusion policies). Distinct from general adversarial robustness — focuses on standardized evaluation infrastructure.
